stories.headline: Trump and Rubio Insist Iran War Is Over, Even as Missiles Fly During Cease-Fire
stories.summary: The White House is turning to rhetorical leaps as President Trump tries to put the biggest political crisis of his presidency behind him.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump says Hormuz operation paused amid US, Tehran talks
stories.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says ceasefire with Iran remains in place despite growing tensions in Hormuz Strait.

stories.headline: Trump says US to pause operation to guide vessels through Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: "Project Freedom", which began on Monday, will be halted because progress has been made toward a deal with Iran, the US president says.

stories.headline: US to Pause Guiding Ships While Seeking Iran Deal: Trump
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he would pause a US-led effort to help stranded ships exit the Strait of Hormuz to see if an agreement with Iran to end the war could be finalized. Bloomberg's Derek Wallbank breaks down the developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump renews feud with Pope Leo XIV days ahead of planned Marco Rubio visit to the Vatican
stories.summary: In an interview with US media outlets, Trump said Pope Leo XIV’s words are helping Iran and making the world “less safe”, slamming him for his stance which Trump perceives to mean indifference about Tehran acquiring nuclear weapons.

stories.headline: Oil prices ease as US pauses Project Freedom to seek deal with Iran
stories.summary: President Donald Trump raised hopes of an agreement between the US and Iran after days of escalation.

stories.headline: Netanyahu’s West Bank plan: annexation by law
stories.summary: Far-right Israeli government uses bureaucracy to cement takeover of Palestinian land

stories.headline: Vance Campaigns in Iowa as G.O.P. Fears Rise Ahead of Midterms
stories.summary: The vice president acknowledged economic headwinds, including rising energy and fertilizer costs. “We got a little — a little blip in the Middle East,” he said, referring to the war in Iran.

stories.headline: Romania’s pro-EU government ousted after no-confidence vote
stories.summary: The pro-European Union coalition of Romanian Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan has collapsed after a vote of no confidence.

stories.headline: What is Trump's 'Project Freedom' in the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: The US president said the operation has been paused after starting on Monday.

stories.headline: IS families in Syria have booked tickets home to Australia, minister says
stories.summary: The four women and nine children with links to Islamic State have spent years in a Syrian camp.

stories.headline: Chinese Metals Demand Gets Lift as Mideast War Spurs Export Boom
stories.summary: Metal exports from China, especially aluminum, are getting a significant lift from the war in the Middle East, which has cut regional supplies while boosting demand for clean-tech products as fossil fuel prices soar.

stories.headline: Ex-FedEx Driver Who Killed 7-Year-Old Athena Strand Is Sentenced to Death
stories.summary: Tanner Horner kidnapped and strangled the girl, Athena Strand, after delivering a package to her home in Texas. The package was her Christmas gift, a set of Barbies.

stories.headline: G.O.P. Proposes $1 Billion in Immigration Bill for Trump’s Ballroom Project
stories.summary: The money would go toward security improvements as part of an East Wing construction project, including a new ballroom that President Trump has said would be built with private dollars.

stories.headline: JD Vance makes his Iowa debut as he campaigns for GOP congressman
stories.summary: The state will play a key role if the vice president decides to run for president in 2028, and other potential GOP candidates have visited in recent months.

stories.headline: State Fund IPO Opens Door to Buy Into Uzbekistan’s Growth Story
stories.summary: Uzbekistan is kicking off a planned series of initial public offerings with the sale of about a 30% stake in the state investment fund, as part of the Central Asian nation’s push to entice international investors.

stories.headline: Indian Small-Caps Rally 20% From March Lows to Near Bull Market
stories.summary: India’s small-cap stocks are staging a sharp comeback, underscoring a revival in risk appetite among investors after months of steep losses.

stories.headline: Asia Central Banks Expected to Move to Higher Interest Rates: JPMorgan AM
stories.summary: Jason Pang of JPMorgan Asset Management says that "higher oil for longer will translate into a more hawkish bias" among central banks in Asia. He also tells Bloomberg Television that "Malaysia, local, remains a good exposure for investors." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: CATL Bears Retreat in Hong Kong After Blockbuster Share Sale
stories.summary: Traders are swiftly unwinding bearish positions against Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd.’s Hong Kong-listed shares as the focus shifts to the battery giant’s growth outlook following a $5 billion share sale.

stories.summary: Small investors own fewer stocks directly, pour money into the market via mutual funds.

stories.headline: Indonesia Tightens Rule on FX Purchases After Rupiah Hit New Record Low
stories.summary: Indonesia further tightened rules on dollar purchases to support the beleaguered rupiah after the currency slumped to a series of record lows.

stories.summary: Global lenders to Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. are weighing multiple financing options for its proposed $12 billion acquisition of New York-listed healthcare company Organon & Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Russian soldiers rehearse Victory Day parade amid fears of Ukrainian attacks

stories.headline: Kyiv slams Egypt for accepting Russian‑shipped grain stolen from Ukraine

stories.headline: Gulf braces itself for potential escalation with Iran

stories.headline: Nine coal miners die in gas explosion in Colombia

stories.headline: EU split over market supervision threatens competitiveness plans
stories.summary: Despite intensifying debate in Brussels on reaching agreement over more harmonised rules for capital markets supervision, member states remain divided, risking delays to Europe’s competitiveness agenda.
